Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Motor Vehicle Safety Act and the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999
Identification.ShortTitle: Ensuring Safe Vehicles Imported from Mexico for Canadians
Identification.RunningHead: Motor Vehicle Safety and Canadian Environmental Protection, 1999

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Motor Vehicle Safety Act and the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 to allow, subject to certain conditions, the importation of certain used vehicles from Mexico in order for Canada to meet its free trade obligations.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Ensuring Safe Vehicles Imported from Mexico for Canadians Act.
The definition vehicle in section 2 of the Motor Vehicle Safety Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: vehicle means any vehicle that belongs to a prescribed class of vehicles.

Section.Text: Subsection 7(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vehicle from United States or Mexico
Subsection.Text: Despite sections 5 and 6, a vehicle that has been sold at the retail level in the United States, or that is a prescribed vehicle from Mexico, and that fails to satisfy an applicable requirement of those sections may be imported into Canada if the vehicle satisfies the conditions specified in the regulations, if any, and if the person importing the vehicle makes a declaration in the prescribed form and manner that,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the prescribed period, the vehicle will be made to conform to that requirement and will be inspected in accordance with the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: before the vehicle is presented for licensing under the laws of a province, the vehicle will be certified in accordance with the regulations to so conform by any person who is designated by the regulations.

Section.Text: Subsection 155(2) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vehicle from United States or Mexico
Subsection.Text: Sections 153 and 154 do not apply in respect of the importation of a vehicle that has been sold at the retail level in the United States, or that is a prescribed vehicle from Mexico, if the vehicle satisfies the conditions specified in the regulations, if any, and if the person importing it makes a declaration in the prescribed form and manner that,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: within the prescribed period, the applicable requirements of those sections will be met and, if required by the regulations, the vehicle will be inspected in accordance with them; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: before the vehicle is presented for licensing under the laws of a province or an aboriginal government, the vehicle will be certified, in accordance with the regulations, as conforming to the applicable requirements of those sections.

Section.Text: Subsection 160(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting inspection and certification referred to in subsection 155(2) and, in particular, designating any person who is authorized to inspect or certify vehicles; and

Section.Text: The provisions of this Act come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
